signals.headline: Russia Declares Temporary 32-Hour Ceasefire with Ukraine for Orthodox Easter | YES | HIGH confidence** [5][6]
signals.raw:
Russia Declares Temporary 32-Hour Ceasefire with Ukraine for Orthodox Easter | YES | HIGH confidence** [5][6]  
Moscow announced a unilateral theater-wide ceasefire effective from April 11, 2026, at 1600 local time, coinciding with the Orthodox Easter holiday to allow religious services.[5][6] Skepticism persists on whether it will hold or lead to broader talks, with ongoing Russia-Ukraine strikes

signals.headline: Chris Dudley Opts Out of Key Oregon Governor Debate, Citing Rival's Absence** | NO | HIGH confidence[3]
signals.raw:
Chris Dudley Opts Out of Key Oregon Governor Debate, Citing Rival's Absence** | NO | HIGH confidence[3]

Chris Dudley, a leading Republican candidate for Oregon governor in 2026 and 2010 nominee who nearly won, declined a major debate hosted by The Oregonian and KGW on April 30, 2026, unless rival Christine Drazan participates; Drazan opted out for other GOP events.[3] No White House involvement,

signals.headline: US Denies Formal Request to Extend Iran Ceasefire | NO | HIGH confidence** [4]
signals.raw:
US Denies Formal Request to Extend Iran Ceasefire | NO | HIGH confidence** [4]  
The White House explicitly denied reports of formally requesting an extension of the fragile US-Iran truce, labeling them "not true at this moment," amid ongoing mediation efforts by Pakistan.[4] This comes as Pakistan's army chief met Iran's foreign minister in Tehran to prevent renewed conflict.[4]

signals.headline: Goldman Sachs Submits Bitcoin ETF Filing with Options Strategy | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Goldman Sachs Submits Bitcoin ETF Filing with Options Strategy | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Goldman Sachs has filed for a Bitcoin-focused ETF emphasizing income via options on spot Bitcoin ETFs, targeting stable markets; this marks fresh institutional momentum amid BTC at ~$74,000-$75,000[1][4].  
Ethereum trades at $2,343 (up slightly today, down YTD), with no major new confirmations[1][2][3].  
T

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Benchmark Interest Rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Benchmark Interest Rate at 2.5% for 7th Consecutive Month (April 10, 2026)** | YES | HIGH  
The Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board unanimously decided to keep the policy rate unchanged at 2.5%, citing inflationary pressures above the 2% target (recent CPI at ~2.2%), rising oil prices from Middle East conflict, and won weakening to ~1,520/USD, signaling no near-term rate cuts
